headline_seed: The federal judge presiding over the White House ballroom case has clarified his ruling to say that security-related work can go on, particularly below ground, but that work on the ballroom itself still cannot proceed without authorization from lawmakers.
draft_text:
Judge Leon split the ruling: below-ground work on national security facilities at the $400M White House ballroom site can continue; above-ground ballroom construction stays blocked.

AP and CBS News confirm the Justice Department filed an appeal Thursday.

And that's the mews.
